name: Aluminum
summary: GULF PRODUCTION UNDER DUAL THREAT: LME at $3,422/mt (-0.9% 1D, +4.3% monthly, +13.7% 3M). Borouge — world's largest single-site polyolefin plant — shut down in UAE from interception debris (April 5). ALBA in Bahrain struck earlier, 19% capacity still shut. Gulf aluminum/petrochemical production facing both direct strike risk AND interception-debris risk. ~9% of global aluminum production at risk.

current.snapshot_note: As of Apr 6 — below March 31 peak of $3,500 but elevated. Dual risk: direct strikes + interception debris.

signals.content: IRGC claimed attacks on ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum facilities in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants. ALBA confirmed the attack — two employees injured. EGA's Al Taweelah site sustained significant damage, six people injured. ALBA had already initiated 'controlled and safe shutdown' of ~19% capacity (Lines 1-3) due to Hormuz-related disruptions. EGA's Al Taweelah produced 1.6 million tonnes of cast metal in 2025. Gulf aluminum output represents ~9% of global production. (Sources: The National Abu Dhabi, Al Jazeera, AlCircle, Business Standard, Gulf Business)

LME aluminum surged to $3,492-$3,500/mt — four-year high — following the ALBA/EGA attacks. Iranian attacks on UAE and Bahrain aluminum plants have heightened supply risks. The National reported aluminum prices 'likely to remain high' as long as Gulf disruption risks persist. This is a new category in Iran's targeting expansion: military → energy → industrial (aluminum). Aluminum is critical input for auto manufacturing, construction, aerospace, and packaging. India imports significant aluminum — cost pressure on Indian auto and construction sectors. (Sources: The National, LME data, Financial Content/MarketMinute)

Aluminum LME at $3,422/mt (-0.9% 1D, +4.3% monthly, +13.7% 3M). Borouge — the world's largest single-site polyolefin plant — shut down in UAE from interception debris on April 5. ALBA aluminum plant in Bahrain was struck earlier in the war and 19% capacity remains shut. Gulf aluminum/petrochemical production now facing DUAL threat: direct IRGC strike risk AND collateral damage from missile interception debris. This expands the risk profile — even successful air defense creates industrial casualties. ~9% of global aluminum production at risk across Gulf facilities. (Sources: The National, LME, Al Jazeera)

edges.context: IRGC struck ALBA and EGA in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants. New industrial targeting category. Interception debris adds collateral damage risk even without direct hits.
ALBA (Aluminium Bahrain) — 1.62M mt/year. 19% capacity still shut. Two workers injured in IRGC attack.
EGA (Emirates Global Aluminium) — Al Taweelah produced 1.6M mt in 2025. Significant damage from IRGC attack. Borouge shut from interception debris April 5. Dual risk profile.
India imports aluminum. LME at $3,422/mt increases input costs for Indian auto (Tata Motors, Maruti), construction, and packaging sectors.

trigger_points.condition: Gulf aluminum production drops >25% sustained (ALBA + EGA combined)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would significantly tighten global aluminum supply. LME could hit $4,000+. Indian auto and construction sectors face margin compression. Hindalco (India's largest aluminum company) could benefit as domestic producer but faces higher energy costs.
